title: coprosma longiflora
intro: Coprosma longiflora, also known as the New Zealand button bush or buttonwood, is a small, flowering shrub native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues and relieving respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and berries of Coprosma longiflora can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
